reviews.reason: The retained pre-v4 capture covered the protocol, constructor, and boundary ID producers but omitted the structural nonzero-field producer; the final production scan deliberately includes that inventoried producer.

The three former findings were validation-error paths that returned the invalid request value; the service now returns a zero Request on validation failure, so no unvalidated request crosses the boundary.

One production cast now reuses the already validated lock path; the other fourteen were under the configured internal/testutil exclusion. This is a production-scope correction plus one code fix, not category suppression.
The six former casts now retain validated typed locals or optional typed wrappers through the checked path, eliminating pointer, named-return, and file-set identity loss without suppressions.
